roadmapSummary: P0 reproducibility and CI roadmap to restore generated runtime parity, enforce dist freshness and adapter CI gates, and document generated artifact release policy.

sizingDecision.rationale: The scope is a P0 harness trust fix, but the work has three distinct review surfaces: generated runtime parity, deterministic CI/check enforcement, and release/documentation policy. Keeping generated dist changes separate from logic and docs reduces review risk.
splitTriggers: Generated dist output can create large mechanical diffs that should not hide script or CI logic changes.
CI/check enforcement touches package scripts, fixture execution, and GitHub Actions behavior.
Release/documentation policy is important but should not block the P0 runtime trust patch.
sizingDecision.whyNotFewer: A single sprint would mix generated dist parity, new validation script behavior, CI changes, package dry-run ordering, and documentation policy. That is review-hostile for a harness reliability fix.
sizingDecision.whyNotMore: More than three sprints would over-fragment one cohesive P0 theme. The work has only three real risk classes: generated runtime parity, enforcement, and policy/documentation.
sprintProofs: Sprint 1 isolates REP-001 so reviewers can verify generated runtime parity without also reviewing new CI or docs behavior.
Sprint 2 groups REP-002, REP-003, and REP-004 because all enforce reproducibility through package scripts and CI gates.
Sprint 3 covers REP-005 because generated artifact policy and release docs should follow the technical gates they document.
